title: Probability scoring — pure functions for VIX + time-of-day + day-of-week adjustments
acceptance_criteria: scoreProbability(input: ScoringInput) → ScoringResult exported as a pure function with no I/O
ScoringInput type: { rawExhaustionScore: number; signalType: 'MOMENTUM_EXHAUSTION'|'SCHEDULED'|'PULLBACK'; currentVix: number|null; signalTimeMs: number; clock: Clock }
ScoringResult type: { rawProbability: number; adjustedProbability: number; confidenceTier: 'HIGH'|'MEDIUM'|'LOW' }
rawProbability = baseProbability derived from rawExhaustionScore: exhaustionScore * 0.40 + 0.35 (linear mapping: score 0→prob 0.35, score 1→prob 0.75)
VIX adjustment: if currentVix is null, no adjustment (do not penalise unavailable data); if vix <= 15: +0.02; if vix > 15: -(vix-15) * 0.005 capped at -0.10
Time-of-day adjustment: 09:20–09:45 IST → +0.05 (early session premium decay phase); 14:00–15:00 IST → -0.05 (low-volume afternoon); else 0
Day-of-week adjustment: Monday → -0.03 (gap risk); Friday → -0.03 (expiry week fear); else 0

SCHEDULED signals are assigned a fixed adjustedProbability = 0.60 (above Scanner's 0.50 threshold but below Precision's 0.70 — correct by design for filter routing). rawProbability = 0.60, no adjustments applied
PULLBACK signals use the same adjustment formula as MOMENTUM_EXHAUSTION but start with baseProbability = 0.60 regardless of rawExhaustionScore

Unit tests cover: probability clamping at 0 and 1; each adjustment direction is correct (high VIX lowers, early session raises, Monday/Friday lower); SCHEDULED always returns 0.60 regardless of other inputs; PULLBACK uses base 0.60 with adjustments applied
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
